text:
She wandered into the empty house. Every window had been closed tight. It was quiet. She'd spent the evening studying her history book. The fire she'd lit outside dwindled low. There was only one chair.

She sighed and settled down. Her foot was bare, damp. Its own candle was long since extinguished. It burned dimly within her old sock. She'd always known the woods behind her house.

She watched the shadows dance. Every now and then their shapes broke apart – rabbits racing through the underbrush, birds flitting out of reach.

Her thoughts went back to how she'd left the world behind so long ago. Her family hadned left for the coast for warmth. They built themselves a cottage miles away.

She imagined them all now. Her mother knitting, her father fishing daily. How different places were.

Out there, trees could be cut down for building. For warmth.

Maybe tomorrow.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
